examples.sentence: The news about the invention spread rapidly the world over, reaching even the most remote villages.
examples.translation: The news about the invention spread rapidly throughout the world, reaching even the most remote villages.

examples.sentence: She dreams of traveling the world over to experience different cultures firsthand.
examples.translation: She dreams of traveling throughout the world to experience different cultures firsthand.

examples.sentence: Reports of the disease outbreak were monitored the world over by international health organizations.
examples.translation: Reports of the disease outbreak were monitored throughout the world by international health organizations.

examples.sentence: The festival is celebrated the world over with various regional customs and traditions.
examples.translation: The festival is celebrated throughout the world with various regional customs and traditions.

examples.sentence: Throughout their marriage, they stuck together through thick and thin, supporting each other no matter what.
examples.translation: Throughout their marriage, they stuck together in all circumstances, including good and bad times, supporting each other no matter what.

examples.sentence: If you want true friends, you have to be there for them through thick and thin.
examples.translation: If you want true friends, you have to be there for them in all circumstances, including both good times and bad.

examples.sentence: Despite losing his job, Tom remained loyal to the company through thick and thin.
examples.translation: Despite losing his job, Tom remained loyal to the company in all circumstances, including difficult times.

examples.sentence: Even when the business was failing, Sarah's team stood by her through thick and thin.
examples.translation: Even when the business was failing, Sarah's team offered steadfast loyalty and support despite adversity.

examples.sentence: You can count on Mike to support you through thick and thin; he's never abandoned a friend.
examples.translation: You can count on Mike to support you steadfastly despite hardship or adversity; he's never abandoned a friend.

examples.sentence: Their friendship has lasted because they've supported each other through thick and thin.
examples.translation: Their friendship has lasted because they've offered each other steadfast loyalty despite hardships.

examples.sentence: We promised to stay together through thick and thin, no matter what challenges we faced.
examples.translation: We promised to stay together in all circumstances, including good and bad times, regardless of difficulties.

examples.sentence: The community showed resilience through thick and thin during the long drought.
examples.translation: The community showed resilience in all circumstances, including both good and bad times, during the long drought.

examples.sentence: Through thick and thin, the volunteers continued their work to help the homeless.
examples.translation: In all circumstances, including good and bad times, the volunteers continued their work to help the homeless.

examples.sentence: She stayed by his side through thick and thin, offering comfort and strength.
examples.translation: She stayed by his side in all circumstances, both good and bad, offering comfort and strength.

examples.sentence: The storm hit the coast thick and threefold, causing damage unlike anything seen before.
examples.translation: The storm hit the coast with great abundance and severity, causing damage unlike anything seen before.

examples.sentence: When the debts piled up, the troubles came thick and threefold on his shoulders.
examples.translation: When the debts piled up, the troubles came in great multiplicity and severity upon him.

examples.sentence: The praise she received was thick and threefold, reflecting her outstanding efforts.
examples.translation: The praise she received was abundant and intense, reflecting her outstanding efforts.

examples.sentence: This fabric is thick and threefold, designed to keep you warm even in the harshest winters.
examples.translation: This fabric has a thickness composed of three layers, designed to keep you warm even in the harshest winters.

examples.sentence: The walls of the ancient fortress were thick and threefold, providing excellent defense.
examples.translation: The walls of the ancient fortress had a thickness made up of three layers, providing excellent defense.

examples.sentence: He wore a coat that was thick and threefold, perfect for the bitter cold outside.
examples.translation: He wore a coat that was threefold in thickness, perfect for the bitter cold outside.

examples.sentence: The book's pages were thick and threefold, making it quite heavy to carry around.
examples.translation: The book's pages were composed of three layers, making it quite heavy to carry around.

examples.sentence: Ever since college, Jake and Sam have been thick as thieves, sharing every secret and adventure.
examples.translation: Ever since college, Jake and Sam have been very close friends, sharing every secret and adventure.

examples.sentence: You could tell the siblings were thick as thieves by the way they laughed and confided in each other.
examples.translation: You could tell the siblings were very close and affectionate by the way they laughed and confided in each other.

examples.sentence: Though they met just last year, the two became thick as thieves almost instantly.
examples.translation: Though they met just last year, the two became very close and friendly almost instantly.

examples.sentence: The two executives were thick as thieves, working secretly to manipulate the market to their advantage.
examples.translation: The two executives were closely allied in secretive behavior, cooperating tightly with shared wrongdoing.

examples.sentence: Rumors suggested that the politicians were thick as thieves, plotting behind closed doors.
examples.translation: Rumors suggested that the politicians were closely allied in conspiratorial behavior, plotting secretly.

examples.sentence: Those two journalists seemed thick as thieves, often sharing leaked information before publication.
examples.translation: Those two journalists seemed closely allied in secretive cooperation, often sharing leaked information.

examples.sentence: The two best friends were thick as thieves, always seen together and sharing their joys and sorrows.
examples.translation: The two best friends were very close and friendly, always seen together and sharing their feelings.

examples.sentence: In the small town, the neighbors were thick as thieves, helping one another at every opportunity.
examples.translation: In the small town, the neighbors were very close and affectionate, helping one another frequently.

examples.sentence: The children have been thick as thieves since they started school, inseparable at recess.
examples.translation: The children have been very close friends since they started school, inseparable at recess.

examples.sentence: Despite their different backgrounds, the team members grew thick as thieves over the season.
examples.translation: Despite their different backgrounds, the team members grew very close and friendly over the season.

examples.sentence: During the crisis, Maria was right in the thick of things, coordinating the emergency response.
examples.translation: During the crisis, Maria was in the middle of the central activity, coordinating the emergency response.

examples.sentence: If you want to understand the project better, you need to be in the thick of things rather than on the sidelines.
examples.translation: If you want to understand the project better, you need to be actively involved in the central part of the situation rather than on the sidelines.

examples.sentence: He found himself in the thick of things when the debate between the two groups escalated suddenly.
examples.translation: He found himself actively involved in the central part of the situation when the debate escalated.

examples.sentence: The reporter was literally in the thick of things, standing amidst the bustling crowd at the festival.
examples.translation: The reporter was literally in the middle of the activity, standing amidst the bustling crowd at the festival.

examples.sentence: When the fire broke out, firefighters were in the thick of things, battling flames inside the building.
examples.translation: When the fire broke out, firefighters were literally in the central part of the scene, battling flames inside the building.

examples.sentence: At the concert, Lisa found herself in the thick of things, surrounded by excited fans.
examples.translation: At the concert, Lisa was literally in the middle of the activity, surrounded by excited fans.

examples.sentence: He stepped into the thick of things just as the meeting reached its most heated point.
examples.translation: He stepped literally into the central part of the situation just as the meeting became most intense.

examples.sentence: After years of public speaking, she developed a thick skin and no longer worries about criticism.
examples.translation: After years of public speaking, she developed emotional resilience and no longer worries about criticism.

examples.sentence: You'll need a thick skin if you want to work in customer service, as complaints are common.
examples.translation: You'll need emotional resilience to work in customer service, as complaints are common.

examples.sentence: He didn't have a thick skin at first, but the constant feedback eventually made him tougher.
examples.translation: He wasn't emotionally resilient at first, but the constant feedback eventually made him tougher.

examples.sentence: The rhinoceros has naturally thick skin that protects it from predators and harsh environments.
examples.translation: The rhinoceros has literally thick or coarse skin that protects it from harm.

examples.sentence: Patients with certain dermatological conditions develop unusually thick skin on their hands and feet.
examples.translation: Patients with some medical conditions develop skin that is physically thick or coarse on their extremities.

examples.sentence: The leather used for the boots is made from thick skin to ensure durability and protection.
examples.translation: The leather used for the boots is made from physically thick animal skin to ensure durability.

examples.sentence: Elephants have thick skin that helps regulate their body temperature and offers protection.
examples.translation: Elephants have physically thick skin that helps regulate body temperature and offers protection.

examples.sentence: The prophecy spoke of a coming thief in the night, an event that would change everything without warning.
examples.translation: The prophecy spoke of an unexpected and sudden arrival that would change everything without warning.

examples.sentence: Many people fear the thief in the night, referring to sudden disasters that happen without notice.
examples.translation: Many people fear sudden and unexpected events that occur without warning.

examples.sentence: The sudden collapse of the company was like a thief in the night, taking investors by surprise.
examples.translation: The sudden collapse was an unexpected and sudden event that caught investors off guard.

examples.sentence: The thief in the night broke into several homes while the neighborhood slept peacefully.
examples.translation: The burglar acted furtively at night, breaking into homes while people slept.

examples.sentence: Police have been searching for the thief in the night who stole valuables from multiple houses.
examples.translation: Authorities are searching for the person who steals at night from multiple houses.

examples.sentence: At night, the thief in the night moves silently, avoiding detection as he targets his victims.
examples.translation: At night, the nocturnal thief moves stealthily to avoid being caught.

examples.sentence: Local residents reported hearing footsteps and suspected a thief in the night lurking nearby.
examples.translation: Local residents heard noises and suspected a burglar operating during the night.

examples.sentence: The layoffs happened like a thief in the night, with no prior warning to the employees.
examples.translation: The layoffs occurred suddenly and unexpectedly without any prior notice.

examples.sentence: She left the party like a thief in the night, slipping away before anyone noticed.
examples.translation: She departed the party stealthily and without notification before anyone noticed.

examples.sentence: The funds disappeared like a thief in the night, leaving the organization in financial trouble.
examples.translation: The funds vanished suddenly and without explanation, causing problems for the organization.

examples.sentence: Everyone laughed loudly at Jim's story; it was a real thigh-slapper.
examples.translation: Everyone laughed loudly at Jim's story; it was an extremely funny joke.

examples.sentence: That comedy show last night was full of thigh-slappers that kept the audience roaring with laughter.
examples.translation: That comedy show last night was full of extremely funny jokes that kept the audience laughing.

examples.sentence: Grandpa's old anecdotes are always thigh-slappers, no matter how many times we've heard them.
examples.translation: Grandpa's old stories are always very funny jokes, no matter how often we hear them.

examples.sentence: His attempt at humor was called a thigh-slapper, but no one found it funny at all.
examples.translation: His joke was presented as funny but was actually unimpressive and not amusing.

examples.sentence: She gave a sarcastic smile, calling his lame joke a real thigh-slapper.
examples.translation: She ironically called his poor joke a 'thigh-slapper' to indicate it was not funny.

examples.sentence: The comedian's joke bombed, and the audience's silence made it clear it wasn't a thigh-slapper.
examples.translation: The comedian's joke was not funny and did not provoke laughter.

examples.sentence: The play was full of thigh-slappers that had the crowd laughing throughout the evening.
examples.translation: The play contained many extremely funny jokes that caused continuous laughter.

examples.sentence: Whenever Bob tells a story, it's always a thigh-slapper; everyone bursts out laughing.
examples.translation: Whenever Bob tells a story, it is very funny and provokes loud laughter.

examples.sentence: The comedian's routine was a series of thigh-slappers that kept the room entertained for hours.
examples.translation: The comedian's routine was full of extremely funny jokes that entertained the audience.

examples.sentence: That joke about the parrot was a real thigh-slapper; I couldn't stop laughing.
examples.translation: That parrot joke was extremely funny and made me laugh a lot.

examples.sentence: The magician appeared to make the coin float in thin air, astonishing the audience.
examples.translation: The magician made the coin appear to float unsupported in empty space, astonishing the audience.

examples.sentence: Hanging from the cliff, it felt like he was suspended in thin air with nothing to hold on to.
examples.translation: Hanging from the cliff, it felt like he was suspended in an empty atmosphere without physical support.

examples.sentence: The drone hovered silently in thin air above the forest canopy.
examples.translation: The drone hovered unsupported in the atmosphere above the forest.

examples.sentence: The crystal chandelier seemed to be floating in thin air, held by nearly invisible wires.
examples.translation: The chandelier appeared to float unsupported in empty space, held by almost invisible wires.

examples.sentence: The important documents vanished into thin air just before the meeting started.
examples.translation: The important documents disappeared completely and inexplicably without trace before the meeting.

examples.sentence: When the money disappeared into thin air, the company was left with no explanation for the loss.
examples.translation: When the money vanished without explanation, the company had no clue about the cause.

examples.sentence: He claimed to have seen the artifact one moment, and then it vanished into thin air.
examples.translation: He claimed to have seen the artifact, but then it completely disappeared without trace.

examples.sentence: They expected the business to survive on thin air, hoping customers would just appear out of nowhere.
examples.translation: They expected the business to exist or survive on insufficient or imaginary means, hoping for customers to come unexpectedly.

examples.sentence: Living on thin air is impossible; you need resources and support to thrive.
examples.translation: Surviving without sufficient resources or support is impossible.

examples.sentence: The plan seemed to rely on thin air, with no solid evidence or backing.
examples.translation: The plan appeared to lack substance or support and was based on imaginary or insufficient means.

examples.sentence: Allowing students to skip homework might be the thin end of the wedge leading to a decline in discipline.
examples.translation: Allowing students to skip homework is a small initial action likely to lead to larger undesirable consequences.

examples.sentence: Cutting corners on safety is the thin end of the wedge that could result in serious accidents.
examples.translation: Cutting corners on safety is a small first step that likely leads to bigger problems.

examples.sentence: Once they start censoring the media, it will be the thin end of the wedge for freedom of speech.
examples.translation: Once media censorship begins, it will be a small initial action likely to cause larger undesirable effects on free speech.

examples.sentence: The new policy change might seem minor but could be the thin end of the wedge for more extensive regulations.
examples.translation: The minor policy change may set a precedent for more significant and problematic developments.

examples.sentence: This small budget cut is just the thin end of the wedge, and more severe reductions are expected.
examples.translation: The small budget cut is a seemingly minor change that could lead to more serious cuts.

examples.sentence: Allowing this exception could become the thin end of the wedge, opening doors to further compromises.
examples.translation: Allowing this exception might be the small initial step that leads to bigger concessions.

examples.sentence: The wedge’s thin end slipped into the crack, starting the process of splitting the wood.
examples.translation: The small initial part of the wedge entered the crack, beginning the splitting of the wood.

examples.sentence: He carefully inserted the thin end of the wedge to pry open the door without damaging it.
examples.translation: He inserted the small initial part of the wedge to open the door without damage.

examples.sentence: The carpenter showed us how the thin end of the wedge is designed to gradually widen the gap.
examples.translation: The carpenter explained that the small initial part of the wedge is made to gradually increase the separation.

examples.sentence: By hitting the thin end of the wedge, the log split cleanly into two halves.
examples.translation: By applying force to the small initial part of the wedge, the log was split neatly.

examples.sentence: Using floppy disks is a thing of the past now that cloud storage is widely available.
examples.translation: Using floppy disks is something that no longer exists or is no longer practiced.

examples.sentence: Manual typewriters have become a thing of the past with the advent of computers.
examples.translation: Manual typewriters are no longer used and belong to a former time.

examples.sentence: Sending letters by post is almost a thing of the past in the digital age.
examples.translation: Sending letters by mail is now an obsolete practice due to digital communication.

examples.sentence: The practice of using horse-drawn carriages is a thing of the past in most cities.
examples.translation: The use of horse-drawn carriages has been discontinued and replaced.

examples.sentence: For many, rotary phones are simply a thing of the past, replaced by smartphones.
examples.translation: Rotary phones are no longer current and have been replaced by modern technology.

examples.sentence: Traditional film cameras have become a thing of the past since digital cameras became popular.
examples.translation: Traditional film cameras are obsolete due to the popularity of digital cameras.

examples.sentence: The old trade routes are now a thing of the past, replaced by modern highways.
examples.translation: The old trade routes no longer exist or are no longer practiced; they are obsolete.

examples.sentence: With the introduction of electric lighting, gas lamps quickly became a thing of the past.
examples.translation: Gas lamps became obsolete and were no longer used after electric lighting was introduced.

examples.sentence: Payphones have largely become a thing of the past as mobile phones dominate communication.
examples.translation: Payphones are mostly obsolete and no longer widely used because of mobile phones.

examples.sentence: The tradition of hand-written invitations is a thing of the past for many modern weddings.
examples.translation: The practice of sending hand-written invitations is no longer common and considered outdated.
